data.content: The build confirms the file is generated but not compiled. The `GenerateSampleCode` target writes the file but never adds it to the `Compile` item group. MSBuild's default `**/*.cs` glob is evaluated at project load time — before any targets run — so it misses files created during the build.

arguments.new_str:
<Message Importance="high" Text="Generated code file: $(_GeneratedFilePath)" />

    <!-- Add generated file to compilation since default globs are evaluated before targets run -->
    <ItemGroup>
      <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
      <FileWrites Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
    </ItemGroup>
  </Target>

data.reasoningText:
The issue is clear. The `GenerateSampleCode` target generates a `.cs` file during the build (before `CoreCompile`), but it doesn't add the generated file to the `Compile` item group. MSBuild's default glob for `*.cs` files is evaluated at project load time, before any targets run, so files created during the build won't be picked up automatically.

The fix is to add the generated file to the `<Compile>` item group within the target, and also add it to `<FileWrites>` so it gets cleaned properly.

Let me use the skill for this since it's specifically about generated files not being compiled.

arguments.new_str:
<Message Importance="high" Text="Generated code file: $(_GeneratedFilePath)" />

    <!-- Add generated file to compilation since default globs are evaluated before targets run -->
    <ItemGroup>
      <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
      <FileWrites Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
    </ItemGroup>
  </Target>

data.content:
Build succeeds now. Here's the diagnosis:

**Problem:** The `GenerateSampleCode` target writes `GeneratedInfo.cs` to the `obj/` directory during the build, but MSBuild's default `**/*.cs` glob is evaluated at **project load time** — before any targets execute. So the file exists on disk but the compiler never sees it.

**Fix:** Added the generated file to the `<Compile>` item group inside the target (so it's included after creation), and to `<FileWrites>` (so `dotnet clean` removes it).
